# Senior AI Agent Engineer
[...]
GE Vernova

- We need 5+ years of relevant experience, along with a Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, AI Engineering, or a related discipline. - We need 3+ years of experience developing and deploying AI or GenAI applications, especially autonomous agent systems or similar AI software engineering work. - We need 3+ years of experience building GenAI solutions that support agent orchestration, multi-step reasoning, prompt design, RAG integration, and model selection. - We need 3+ years of experience working with LLMs and deep learning models, including the machine learning lifecycle, data generation methods, training, and validation. - We need strong programming ability in Python and/or languages such as JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, Java, or Go, with solid software engineering practices. - We need hands-on expertise with AI and ML frameworks and tools such as LangChain, OpenAI APIs, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and commercial or open-source LLMs. - We need practical knowledge of prompt engineering, natural language processing, multi-agent systems, and orchestration platforms such as AutoGen, LangGraph, or MCP. - We need familiarity with data management, vector databases, semantic search, and real-time data pipelines. - We need experience deploying AI solutions on cloud environments such as AWS or Google Cloud using Docker and Kubernetes. - We need a strong understanding of model training, fine-tuning, evaluation, AI ethics, data privacy, and secure information handling. - We need the ability to work independently in fast-paced, experimental settings. - We require applicants to be 18 years or older and legally authorized to work in the United States. - We require candidates to be willing to work from our Niskayuna, NY or Cambridge, MA office locations.
[...]
- We design, build, and tune AI agents using LLMs, reinforcement learning, planning methods, and decision-making frameworks. - We develop scalable multi-agent systems that support long-horizon reasoning, autonomy, interaction, and complex task execution. - We connect AI agents with APIs, backend systems, databases, and enterprise platforms. - We prototype, launch, and support AI-powered solutions while maintaining reliability and performance in production. - We improve agent behavior through ongoing feedback loops, reinforcement learning, and user input. - We work closely with research, engineering, product, and deployment teams to refine capabilities and drive innovation. - We evaluate agent performance, run structured testing, add safety controls, and uphold responsible AI practices. - We document architectures, design choices, workflows, and technical processes thoroughly. - We stay informed about new AI developments, contribute to platform and tooling enhancements, and share knowledge across our team. - We push forward the boundaries of planning, reasoning, and agentic intelligence through large-scale AI and ML solutions.

results.title: GE Vernova Internship 2026 — Digital & Automation Engineering (Apply Now) - Jobscoupe.com
highlights:
GE Vernova Internship 2026 — Digital & Automation Engineering (Apply Now) - Jobscoupe.com

GE Vernova Internship 2026: Apply for GE Vernova Internship 2026 — Digital & Automation Engineering. Paid internship for freshers. Locations: Bengaluru. Apply now — limited seats.

### 🔍 About GE Vernova:

Building on over 130 years of experience, GE Vernova is leading a new era of energy – electrifying the world while simultaneously working to decarbonize it. Addressing the climate crisis is an urgent global priority and we take our responsibility seriously. If we want our energy future to be different…we must be different. Our mission is embedded in our name. We retain our treasured legacy, “GE,” in our name as an enduring and hard-earned badge of quality and ingenuity. “Ver” / “verde” signal Earth’s verdant and lush ecosystems. “Nova,” from the Latin “novus,” nods to a new, innovative era of lower carbon energy that GE Vernova will help deliver.

### 💼 Job Role: Digital & Automation Engineering – Intern

### 📝 Job Description:

- Be a part of team to support development using ML and Generative AI technologies including but not limited to LLMs and Vision Models
- Work effectively on an assigned project in the field of Steam Turbine engineering application development.
- Work closely with the design community to identify business needs, create a problem statement, & develop functional specifications and support software development.
- Work closely with an identified mentor on an allocated project, make presentations.
- Document technical data generated by the assigned project consistent with organization policies and procedures.

### Requirements:

- Working towards Bachelors/master’s in computer science (Specialization in AI/ML and Data Sciences).

### Skills:

- Knowledge of programming languages such as Python with analytics skills and exposure to web services.,
- Understanding of data structures, algorithms and computer architectures with ability to write highly optimized code.
- Experience with data modelling and applied mathematics.
- Experience building models with deep learning frameworks to find solutions to that data that represent approximately to a partial differential equation data.
- Experience development and deploying models with Flask, Django, AWS, Azure, or other cloud services.
- Knowledge of relevant statistical, mathematical, and computational concepts.
- Ability to collaborate with others, work in a team, maintain a respectful working environment.
- Strong written, composed verbal communication, presentation and organization skills.
- Curious disposition that allows you to think both critically and outside the box.
- Interest in learning the latest technologies and methods in this space and orientation to research, find new/unconventional ways to solve complex problems.

### End Date:

- 9 June 2026

### Join Our Telegram Group for Instant Job Notification

Qualification: B.E / B.Tech / M.E / M.Tech

results.title: GE Vernova Digital Technology Development Program (DTDP) – India 2027 | Full Details » Fresher Go
highlights:
GE Vernova Digital Technology Development Program (DTDP) – India 2027 | Full Details » Fresher Go

# GE Vernova Digital Technology Development Program (DTDP) – India 2027 | Full Details

## Build Your Career in Technology with GE Vernova

Looking to start your career in technology with one of the world’s leading industrial and energy technology companies? GE Vernova has opened applications for its Digital Technology Development Program (DTDP) – India 2027, a premium early-career leadership and technical development program.

This is a full-time opportunity in Bangalore designed for students passionate about software, AI, cloud, cybersecurity, data, and technology leadership.

---

## Job Overview

- Courses & Preparation
- Company: GE Vernova
- Role: Digital Technology Development Program (DTDP) – India 2027
- Job ID: R5043600
- Job Type: Full Time
- Location: Bangalore, India
- Program Duration: 2 Years
- Application Start: 1 June 2026
- Application End: 8 June 2026

---

## About the Program

GE Vernova’s DTDP is a structured 2-year rotational leadership program.

Selected candidates will work directly with technology teams and rotate across different departments every six months to gain practical experience.

### Rotation Areas:

- Product & Program Management
- Cyber Security
- Cloud Technology
- Network Technology
- Data Analytics
- Data Science
- Artificial Intelligence
- Engineering
- Business Technology Functions

Participants also receive:

- Leadership training
- Technical coursework
- Global exposure
- Mentorship from senior professionals

---

## Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must have:

### Education

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in:

- Computer Science
- Computer Engineering
- Computer Information Systems
- Management Information Systems
- Data Analytics
- Data Science
- Information Technology
- Software Engineering
- Relevant STEM disciplines with IT experience

### Academic Requirement

- Minimum 3.0/4.0 GPA (or equivalent)

### Graduation Requirement

- Expected graduation by May/June 2027

---

## Preferred Skills

GE Vernova prefers candidates who have:

- Strong analytical skills
- Interest in technology and innovation
- Knowledge of current software trends
- Internship or project experience
- Problem-solving ability
- Understanding of coding standards
- Leadership experience
- Communication skills
- Team collaboration mindset
- Project management exposure

---

## Locations & Mobility

Candidates should be open to relocation.

Possible locations include:

- Bangalore (India)
- Atlanta (USA)
- Greenville (USA)
- Schenectady (USA)
- Cambridge (USA)
- Budapest (Hungary)

Relocation assistance is provided.

---

## Salary / CTC

GE Vernova has not officially disclosed the CTC in the posting.

Estimated range (based on similar global technology graduate programs):₹12–20 LPA (estimate only, not official)

---

## How to Stand Out

If you don’t have strong projects yet:

- Build 2–3 good full-stack projects
- Learn cloud basics (AWS/Azure)
- Practice DSA
- Add one AI/data project
- Keep resume ATS-friendly
- Show leadership or team work experience

---

## Final Thoughts

This is a strong opportunity for students graduating in 2027 who want exposure to software engineering, AI, cloud, analytics, and leadership inside a global company.

Apply early because the posting closes soon.

GE Vernova is a leader in the energy sector with over 130 years of experience, dedicated to electrifying the world while decarbonizing it. The company offers a broad portfolio of energy solutions including gas, hydro, nuclear, and wind power technologies, aimed at providing reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy. With a strong focus on innovation, GE Vernova plays a significant role in reducing the carbon footprint of global power systems and supports the transition to net-zero emissions by 2030.

# Lead Application Engineer – Grid Integration, Stability
[...]
🗽 New York – Remote🗽 New York – Remote
[...]
## GE Vernova
[...]
🤖 Artificial Intelligence
[...]
Energy • Aerospace • Artificial Intelligence
[...]
GE Vernova is a leader in the energy sector with over 130 years of experience, dedicated to electrifying the world while decarbonizing it. The company offers a broad portfolio of energy solutions including gas, hydro, nuclear, and wind power technologies, aimed at providing reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy. With a strong focus on innovation, GE Vernova plays a significant role in reducing the carbon footprint of global power systems and supports the transition to net-zero emissions by 2030.
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Perform technical analysis related to power electronic and power systems control of renewable generation, battery energy storage systems, large industrial drives and FACTS. • Support customers with power systems engineering expertise; including modeling, analysis, equipment design and specification, and failure mode analysis • Create, modify and parametrize simulation models representing GE Vernova inverter-based resource (IBR) products like wind turbines, solar and battery storage. • Evaluate grid code compliance standards and their impact on renewable energy projects in bulk power systems. • Support development of transmission and distribution connected IBR and distributed energy resource (DER) products for evolving codes and standards through participation in industry standards groups and leading innovative approaches to power system analysis. • Perform power system transient studies, and detailed equipment modeling using appropriate software tools. • Effectively communicate results to both internal and external customers by preparing detailed written reports and presentations describing analyses performed, solutions developed, and customer value propositions • Enhance industry knowledge and present technical content in regional forums and conferences; serve as a thought leader in industry activities. • Actively participate in a working environment where teamwork, quality, customer service and innovation are valued. • Willingness to travel for supporting project work, meeting team members, attending industry standard working group meetings and conferences (up to 10%).
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• Ph.D. degree in electrical engineering, electric power engineering, or related field of study – no industry experience is required with this qualification OR Master’s degree in electrical engineering or electric power engineering with 3 years of experience. • Minimum three (3) years of experience performing transient analysis using simulation tools like PSCAD/EMTDC, EMTP, PSS/E, PowerFactory or PSLF. • Minimum three (3) years of experience with the use and application of common programming languages (e.g. Python, MATLAB, FORTRAN).

highlights:
Software Engineer AI/ML in Evendale, Ohio, United States of America | Digital Technology / IT at GE Aerospace Job1

GE Aerospace uses enhanced security screening measures to detect and prevent against fraudulent hires.

About Us

At GE Aerospace, we invent the future of flight, lift people up, and bring them home safely. We are a world-leading provider of jet engines, components, and integrated systems for commercial and military aircraft. At GE Aerospace, respect for people is core to our culture and commitment to FLIGHT DECK, our proprietary lean operating model. We attract, develop, and retain industry-leading talent from all backgrounds and cultivate communities where people are respected and empowered to reach their fullest potential. For the people of GE Aerospace, their work isn't just a job. It's their passion and purpose. We work and win as one team, accountable to each other and our customers, and we are always finding a better way to climb higher together. We were meant to fly.

At GE Aerospace, we value authenticity and genuine interactions during the hiring process. To ensure a fair and transparent evaluation, the use of AI tools to aid in responses, including but not limited to AI-generated answers, real-time assistance, or automated decision-making, is not allowed. Candidates are expected to engage directly and authentically, therefore, unauthorized use of AI tools during interviews may result in disqualification from the hiring process. Note: this restriction does not apply to an individual’s use of AI or technology if required for accessibility or accommodation purposes. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. We look forward to connecting with you!

We are a world-leading provider of jet engines, components, and integrated systems for commercial and military aircraft. At GE Aerospace, respect for people is core to our culture and commitment to FLIGHT DECK, our proprietary lean operating model. We attract, develop, and retain industry-leading talent from all backgrounds and cultivate communities where people are respected and empowered to reach their fullest potential. For the people of GE Aerospace, their work isn't just a job. It's their passion and purpose. We work and win as one team, accountable to each other and our customers, and we are always finding a better way to climb higher together. We were meant to fly.

At GE Aerospace, we value authenticity and genuine interactions during the hiring process. To ensure a fair and transparent evaluation, the use of AI tools to aid in responses, including but not limited to AI-generated answers, real-time assistance, or automated decision-making, is not allowed. Candidates are expected to engage directly and authentically, therefore, unauthorized use of AI tools during interviews may result in disqualification from the hiring process. Note: this restriction does not apply to an individual’s use of AI or technology if required for accessibility or accommodation purposes. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. We look forward to connecting with you!

Having technical issues with this site or our application? We are here to help! CLICK HERE

# AI & LLM Engineering Lead

Posted May 25, 2026

- Job Description:
- Lead AI/LLM strategy, solution architecture, and implementation across Engineering, Operations, and Project Delivery.
- Build and maintain LLM-based agents to support: intelligent processing of technical documentation, automated design validation and engineering workflows, testing and QA automation, knowledge retrieval and contextual reasoning.
- Integrate AI into core power automation workflows: IEC 61850 SCD engineering files, relay settings, SCADA HMI & logic, substation documentation, etc.
- Establish AI governance, secure data pipelines, and compliance with utility-grade cybersecurity standards.
- Partner with engineering managers and subject-matter experts to identify high-value AI automation opportunities.
- Develop scalable pipelines for inference, fine-tuning, continuous learning, and lifecycle management in cloud and on-prem environments.
- Evaluate and incorporate emerging AI technologies (RAG, vector stores, autonomous agents, internal copilots).
- Monitor model performance, accuracy, drift, and cost; lead improvement cycles and risk mitigation.
- Train and coach engineering teams on practical AI tools and adoption in daily workflows.
- Ensure compliance with GE Vernova global standards, regulatory expectations, and utility-sector requirements.

- Requirements:
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field.
- Hands-on experience with AI/ML development and production deployment.
- Proven track record in full product lifecycle: ideation, prototyping, development, operations.
- Industrial, energy, or automation domain experience strongly preferred.
- Deep expertise with: Large Language Models, generative AI, and intelligent agents
- Engineering workflow automation
- Python and modern ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow)
- API-driven solution design and MLOps practices
- Cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP) and on-prem architectures
- Data governance and cybersecurity best practices
- Fluent English and Portuguese required
- Spanish proficiency preferred

- Benefits:
- Relocation Assistance Provided: No

results.title: Engineer, Thermal Hydraulic Analyses | Jobflarely
highlights:
Engineer, Thermal Hydraulic Analyses | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# Engineer, Thermal Hydraulic Analyses

Posted May 26, 2026

GE Vernova is focused on reactor nuclear thermal-hydraulic analysis and methods. The role involves developing expertise in thermal-hydraulic analysis, integrating technologies, and providing technical support for reactor system assessments while ensuring compliance with regulations.

### Responsibilities

- Contribute to fuel design optimization, testing, verification, and regulatory licensing for nuclear reactor performance evaluation
- Advance thermal-hydraulic technologies through testing for system efficiency and safety
- Use CFD for modeling reactor system thermal hydraulics
- Develop programs to support thermal-hydraulic analyses and simulations
- Provide technical support for reactor system assessments, including MCO, leakage, and hydrodynamic analyses
- Ensure compliance with NRC regulations and nuclear quality standards
- Collaborate with international teams across various time zones
- Maintain integrity and accountability with attention to detail
- Work effectively in teams and demonstrate strong interpersonal skills
- Pursue continuous improvement with a positive attitude
- Communicate effectively both orally and in writing
- Apply strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Skills

- Bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college in computational methods, thermal-hydraulics, nuclear engineering, physics, mathematics, or a closely related technical discipline
- This role requires access to U.S. export-controlled information. If applicable, final offers will be contingent on ability to obtain authorization for access to U.S. export-controlled information from the U.S. Government
- Master's degree from an accredited university or college in computational methods, thermal-hydraulics, nuclear engineering, physics, mathematics, or a closely related technical discipline
- Prior experience with thermal-hydraulic methods (TRACG, COBRAG, ISCOR, etc.)
- Prior experience with computational fluid dynamics (CFD) using ANSYS-Fluent or similar
- Experience with software Commercial Grade Dedication in accordance with NQA-1
- Programming experience with Fortran, C#, and Python
- Working knowledge of nuclear reactor core licensing, technical specifications, safety margin bases, code methodology licensing, and related federal regulations
- Clear and demonstrated understanding of nuclear industry and/or energy industry
- Demonstrated ability to make decisions with speed and accuracy based on the best available information
- Great organizational skills and a track record of achieving goals and overcoming challenges
- Six Sigma Green Belt, Black Belt, or equivalent quality certification

### Benefits

- Medical, dental, vision, and prescription drug coverage
- Access to Health Coach from GE Vernova, a 24/7 nurse-based resource
- Access to the Employee Assistance Program, providing 24/7 confidential assessment, counseling and referral services
- GE Vernova Retirement Savings Plan
- Tax-advantaged 401(k) savings opportunity with company matching contributions and company retirement contributions
- Access to Fidelity resources and financial planning consultants
- Tuition assistance
- Adoption assistance
- Paid parental leave
- Disability benefits
- Life insurance
- 12 paid holidays
- Permissive time off

Company Overview

- GE Vernova provides energy consulting, gas power, and grid solutions. It was founded in 2024, and is headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://www.gevernova.com.

Apply for This Job

# Lead Generative AI Specialist
[...]
Are you passionate about improving capabilities, efficiency and performance? Are you interested in responsible for supporting the business owners to lean the process? Join our Digital Technology Team! Our team provides industry-leading products and services that optimize the production and processing of energy. You will contribute to our future as an AI player in the Energy Transformation industry. Partner with the best As a Generative AI Specialist, you will work in teams providing smart solution and addressing problems in a commercial technology and consultancy development environment. In this role, you will contribute to the development and deployment of modern generative AI models, techniques and operational research to leverage the power of data to solve complex problems and push the boundaries of what is possible in the ever-evolving tech landscape. As a Lead Generative AI Specialist, you will be responsible for: Designing and developing of generative AI models, including but not limited to LLMs, VLMs, GANs and Multimodal LLMs. Conducting and driving forward innovative research in the field of generative AI, leading the team in exploring new methodologies and technologies. Working closely with cross-functional teams to integrate new generative models into existing AI systems, ensuring seamless integration and optimizing performance. Developing and testing software components according to customer and technical requirements. Preparing comprehensive reports and presentations on the impact of generative AI initiatives on the business. Fuel your passion T o be successful in this role you will have: Master's degree in computer science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field with a specialization in generative models and proven experience in the development and deployment of generative AI models. PhD is preferred Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across different disciplines. Proven experience in software programming languages, with a focus on Python, including AI libraries eg PyTorch, TensorFlow, Transformer architecture. Demonstrated skill at function in a team setting Demonstrated awareness of critical thinking and problem solving methods Demonstrated awareness of presentation and influencing skills Ability to analyze and resolve problems through innovation and creative solutions. Natural communication skills, with an ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Self-motivation that promotes teamwork and collaboration in a cross-functional environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working remotely from home or any other work location Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Lead Engineer, Mathematics & Data Science, Research and Technology Development
